title: jasminum simplicifolium subsp. australiense
intro: Jasminum simplicifolium subsp. australiense, also known as the Australian native jasmine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Jasminum simplicifolium subsp. australiense include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the flowers or leaves in hot water or a solvent to extract its medicinal compounds.
